assertions.reason: A live relay-bot-wrapper run attached a mock Feishu user, forwarded a 50,010-character agent message through the wrapper and relay, split it into two Feishu-sized chat messages, reconstructed the exact original payload from the delivered chunks, and preserved the full message in relay history without truncation.

On SIGTERM, the real server process did not shut down gracefully and had to be SIGKILLed; the attached bot user received no shutdown notification, although the wrapper continued local stdio proxying and echoed a local post-shutdown prompt.

After the wrapper disconnected into grace period, the offline session still appeared in /list with online:false, the bot rejected /attach for that session with the user-facing message "Session is offline", and no attachment state was created.

commands.command: node --input-type=module (inline relay-bot integration validation script writing cross-edge-live-check.json)
commands.observation: The script ran three isolated mocked end-to-end scenarios: large-message forwarding passed with exact reconstruction and history preservation; offline attach rejection passed with the bot replying 'Session is offline'; graceful shutdown failed because the SIGTERM'd server never exited cleanly, required SIGKILL, and produced no user-facing shutdown message while the wrapper kept local proxying alive.

frictions.description: The dedicated user-testing-flow-validator subagent could not complete this group because Task execution returned no output once and then exited with a permissions error.
frictions.resolution: Completed the missing cross-edge validation inline with a local Node harness and recorded the same style of flow-report/evidence artifacts manually.

summary: Validated the remaining 3 feishu-bot cross-area assertions: VAL-CROSS-019 passed, VAL-CROSS-022 passed, and VAL-CROSS-020 failed because graceful shutdown propagation is not implemented end-to-end.
